titleStmt.title: Gulo Maḵadā, ʿĀddi Qiyāḥto Qǝddǝst Māryām, QQM-005
Gadla ḥawāryāt, Apocryphal Acts of the Apostles

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msContents.summary:
I) Gadla ḥawāryāt, Apocryphal Acts of the Apostles (fols. 2ra-142vb) I-1) Introduction (fols. 2ra-2va) I-2) The Preaching of St Peter (fols. 2va-13rb) I-3) The Martyrdom of St Peter (fols. 13rb-15vb) I-4) The Martyrdom of St Paul (fols. 15vb-17vb) I-5) The Preaching of St Simon the son of Cleopas, surnamed Judas or Nathaniel (fols. 17vb-19rb) I-6) The Martyrdom of St Simon the son of Cleopas, surnamed Judas or Nathaniel (fols. 19rb-20va) I-7) The Preaching of St James the Just (fols. 20va-22ra)
I-8) The Martyrdom of St James the Just (fols. 22ra-25ra) I-9) The Preaching of St Bartholomew (fols. 25rb-30ra) I-10) The Martyrdom of St Bartholomew (fols. 30ra-32vb) I-11) The Acts of St Matthew (fols. 32vb-40vb) I-11) The Martyrdom of St Matthew (fols. 40va-43ra) I-13) The Martyrdom of St Luke (fols. 43ra-46va) I-14) The Preaching of St Philip (fols. 46vb-50vb) I-15) The Martyrdom of St Philip (fols. 51ra-53vb) I-16) The Preaching of St Andrew (fols. 53vb-64rb) I-17) The Martyrdom of St Andrew (fols. 64va-66ra) I-18) The Acts of Sts. Andrew and Bartholomew (fols. 66rb-81vb) I-19) The Acts of St John the Son of Zebedee (fols. 81vb-95ra) I-20) The Death of St John the Son of Zebedee (fols. 95rb-99va) I-21) The Martyrdom of St James the Son of Alphaeus (fols. 99va-100va) I-22) The Preaching of St Matthias (fols. 100va-109rb)
I-23) The Martyrdom of St Matthias (fols. 109rb-111rb) I-24) The Acts of St James the Son of Zebedee (fols. 111rb-114vb) I-25) The Martyrdom of St James the Son of Zebedee (fols. 114vb-116va) I-26) The Martyrdom of St Mark the Evangelist (fols. 116va-120ra) I-27) The Preaching of St Thomas (fols. 120ra-132vb) I-28) The Martyrdom of St Thomas (fols. 133ra-137rb) I-29) The Preaching of St Judas Thaddeus (fols. 137rb-142vb)
Additiones 1-3.

msItem.title: Gadla ḥawāryāt
msItem.note: For each text, the equivalent pages of Budge's Edition (BConAp I, II) and the abbreviations used by Bausi in Annali 2000-2001 are given here following:
I-1) BConAp I, 3-6; BConAp II, 2-3; Int in BauGaH
I-2) BConAp I, 7-36; BConAp II, 5-25; Pt1 in BauGaH
I-3) BConAp I, 37-41, 45-49; BConAp II, 26-34; Pt2 in BauGaH
I-4) BConAp I, 41-45; BConAp II, 35-39; Pa1 in BauGaH
I-5) BConAp I, 67-70; BConAp II, 58-61; Si1 in BauGaH
I-6) BConAp I, 70-72; BConAp II, 62-64; Si2 in BauGaH
I-7) BConAp I, 73-76; BConAp II, 65-68; Jc1 in BauGaH
I-8) BConAp I, 76-82; BConAp II, 69-75; Jc2 in BauGaH
I-9) BConAp I, 83-92; BConAp II, 76-86; Ba1 in BauGaH
I-10) BConAp I,93-100; BConAp II, 87-92; Ba2 in BauGaH
I-11) BConAp I, 101-13; BConAp II,93-108; Mt1 in BauGaH
I-12) BConAp I, 114-18; BConAp II, 109-14; Mt2 in BauGaH
I-13) BConAp I, 119-25; BConAp II, 115-21; Luc in BauGaH
I-14) BConAp I, 126-34; BConAp II, 122-30; Ph1 in BauGaH
I-15) BConAp I, 135-39; BConAp II, 131-36; Ph2 in BauGaH
I-16) BConAp I, 140-56; BConAp II, 137-53; An1 in BauGaH
I-17) BConAp I, 184-88; BConAp II, 181-85; An2 in BauGaH
I-18) BConAp I, 156-83; BConAp II, 154-80; AnB in BauGaH
I-19) BConAp I,189-213; BConAp II,186-211; Jo1 in BauGaH
I-20) BConAp I, 214-22; BConAp II, 212-20; Jo2 in BauGaH
I-21) BConAp I, 223-24; BConAp II, 221-22; JcA in BauGaH
I-22) BConAp I, 225-42; BConAp II, 223-40; Ma1 in BauGaH
I-23) BConAp I,243-246; BConAp II, 241-45; Ma2 in BauGaH
I-24) BConAp I, 247-53; BConAp II, 246-52; JZ1 in BauGaH
I-25) BConAp I, 254-57; BConAp II, 253-56; JZ2 in BauGaH
I-26) BConAp I, 257-64; BConAp II, 257-64; Mar in BauGaH
I-27) BConAp I, 265-87; BConAp II, 265-86; Th1 in BauGaH
I-28) BConAp I, 287-95; BConAp II, 287-95; Th2 in BauGaH
I-29) BConAp I,296-306; BConAp II,296-306; Jud in BauGaH.
- According to the distribution of the texts, the Gadla ḥawāryāt of QQM-005would belong to the I recension’s group (cp. BauGaH, 83-84).
Text I-3, the Martyrdom pf St Peter, does not contain the "Discourse of Peter on the Cross" (= BConAp I, 45-49; BConAp II, 30-34).
- The work ends with the subscriptio (142vb):

ተፈጸመ፡ በዝየ፡ ገድል፡ ዘ፲ወ፪፡ ሓዋርያት፡ ጸሎቶሙ፡ ትዕቀበነ፡ ለዘጸሐፋ፡ ወለአጽሐፋ፡ ለዘአንበባ፡ ለዓለም፡ አሜን፡, “Herewith the (the book of the) Vitae of the twelve Apostles has been completed. May their prayer protect us, the ones who wrote and the ones who caused to write and the one who recited it. Forever. Amen”.
The subscriptio is separated from the main text with black and red dots connected with dashes, and is followed by the same sign.
- Other subscriptiones are in 143rab; see below Additiones 2-3.
- The scribe did not use the bottom ruled line, e.g., in 15v, 22v-23r, 31v-32rv, 35r, 75rv.
- Each text, as well the additional notes after it, is separated by the previous one by a chain of red and black dots and/or a chain of black and red dots and dashes.

supportDesc.extent: Made of 143.0 folios, of which 2.0 blank 1r, 143v, in 19.0 quires . 325 265 95

list.item: 10 I(10/fols. 1r-10v) f. 1 is recent, attached to its counterpart (fol. 10) by means of a parchment guard.

supportDesc.condition: The Ms. was rebound. The original sewing of the quires is missing; most of the quires are reinforced with parchment guards, on the spinefolds or in the centrefols of the quires. The majority of the leaves are affected by humidity at the upper and the bottom edges (esp. the last leaves). Small holes are in several leaves; the last folio is cut at the bottom.

layout.ab: the number of the lines ranges between 25 and 28.
Pricking and ruling are partly visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling.
The bottom line is written above the ruling.

handDesc.handNote: Mediocre Black; red (vermilion). Scribe: Zenā Gabrǝʾel. Zenā Gabrǝʾel is mentioned as the scribe in Additio 2. The handwriting is similar to that of Ms. ATH-001.Mid- 15th to mid 16th century (?) Nomina sacra; a few lines in the incipit of each text, alternating with black lines; the first two lines, alternating with a black line, of the account proper of Text 1-16) and of an internal part of Text I-3 (fol. 15vb); Ethiopic numerals or their elements; elements of the punctuation signs.
The handwriting is upright to slightly left-sloping. Some letters tend to rounded and curved shapes, some others to angular shapes (e.g., the letters ዐ and መ).
- the letter ጰ has the sixth order marker (ጵ) directed to the left (e.g., 38vb l.8, 39ra l.5);

- the letter የ has the left part of the sixth order (ይ) marker very short and directed downwards; its right part directed upwards (e.g., 44vb l.1). The shaft of the letter is sharply slanted to the left.
- the letter የ in the fourth order (ያ) has a shaft slanted to the left, similar to some other letters (ደ).
- in the first order, the letter ደ is slanted to the left, its right leg is longer than the left one.

decoDesc.decoNote: 2r: narrow, simple, coloured (red and black) horizontal ornamental band at the bottom of the incipit page (added later?). Geometric motifs (interlaces and lines).

item.quote: 1v (above): ዛአ፡[sic] አንቀጽ፡ ጻ[ድ]ቃን፡ ይበውዕዋ፡ ወኃጥአን፡ የሐሥሥዋ፡
1v (above): The righteous ones enter this gate and the sinners are looking for it.
item.note: Written in a recent secondary hand (in black and red). At the bottom and at the sides, the note is surrounded by a circle of red and black dots. The form of the letter የ in in the first order, as it appears in the verb የሐሥሥዋ፡, is peculiar; the shaft which usually goes straight down from the left side of the body, starts here from the middle of it (perhaps influenced by the appearance of the letter in the main text)

item.desc: (ls.1-10): Note on the commissioner and supplication prayer.
item.quote: 143ra (ls.1-10): ለዛቲ፡ ገድል፡ አጽሐፋ፡ ሀብተ፡ ሥላሴ፡ ተአሚኖ፡ ዓቢየ፡ ተስፋ፡ በእንተ፡ ጸሎታ፡ ለማርያም፡ ወሊዲተ፡[sic] አልፋ፡ ከመ፡ ይንሣእ፡ በረከተ፡ ሳውል፡ ወኬፋ፡ ወሊተሂ፡ ማርያም፡ ትኩኖ፡ ሓገፋ፡ ምስለ፡ ዜና፡ ገብርኤል፡ ዘጸ[ሐ]ፋ፡ ለዓለም፡ ወለዓለ[፡] ዓለም፡ አሜን። ወአሜን።

143ra (ls.1-10): The one who caused this Vita be written (is) Habta Śǝllāse, having great hope in the prayer of Mary, the Mother of Alfa, so that he might take the blessing of Saul and "Stone". And for me, and for Zenā Gabrǝʾel who wrote it (the book), she will be a protection. Forever and ever. Amen.
item.note: Written in the main hand; the note mentions both the commissioner and the scribe. For Kefa (Jn. 1:43, 1 Cor. 1:12, 15:5), cp. DillmLex 876.

item.desc: (l.12-20-rb): Excommunication for those who illegally took the book or brought it out of the church.
item.quote: 143ra (l.12-20-rb): ለዛቲ፡ ገድል፡ ዘ፲ወ፪፡ አርድእት። እመሂ፡ ዘተሃይደ፡ ወእመሂ፡ ዘአውፅአ፡ እመኮነ፡ ዛቲ፡ ማርያም፡ እመሂ፡ ቀሲስ፡ ወእመሂ፡ ዲያቆን። ወእመሂ፡ ስዩም፡ አውገዝኩ፡ አነ፡ ሀብተ፡ ስላሴ፡ ቀሲስ፡ ወመነኮስ፡ በዘኢይትፈታሕ፡ ማእሰረ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ወጳውሎስ፡ አዕማደ፡ ምድር፡ በአፈ፡ ነቢያት፡ ወሐዋርያት። በአፈ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ልሳናተ፡ እሳት። በአፈ፡ ሚካኤል፡ ወገብርኤል፡ ወኪሩቤል፡ በአፉሃ፡ ለማርያም፡ ወላዲተ፡ አምላክ፡ ይኩን፡ እሱረ፡ በሰማያት፡ ወበምድር፡ ለዓለም፡ አሜን።

143ra (l.12-20-rb): If this (book) of the Vitae of the twelve Apostles has been robbed or brought out of the place of Mary, be it by a priest or a deacon or a governor, I, Habta Śǝllāse, priest and monk, excommunicate (him) through the rope of Peter and Paul, pillars of the world, which will not be loosened, through the mouth of the Prophets and Apostles, the mouth of the metropolitan, tongues of the fire, through Michael and Gabriel and Kirubiel, the mouth of Mary, Mother of God. May it be tied in the heaven and on the earth. For eternity, amen.

list.item: - A short note, partly unreadable: ዕቀባ፡ [sic?] ሥላሴ፡ ወሐይለ፡ ሥላሴ፡ ወከፍለ፡ [n.l.]ም፡ ፍቁራንየ፡ "ʿƎquba Śǝllāse and Ḫāyla Śǝllāse and Kǝfla ... (are) my beloved ones"

item.desc: - Readings are indicated by the commemoration dates of the Apostles, written in the main hand, in red, in the upper margin: አመ፡ ፲ለሐምሌ፡ ምንባብ፡: fol. 17v አመ፲ወ፰ለሐምሌ።: fol. 19r አመ፡ ፩ለመስከረም፡ ምንባብ፡: fol. 25r አመ፡ ፳፪፡ ለጥቅምት።: fol. 45r አመ፡ ፲ወ፷ለኅደር፡ምንባብ፡: fol. 46v አመ፡ ፬ለታሕሣስ፡ ምንባብ፡: fol. 53v አመ፡ ፬፡ ለጥር፡: fol. 81v አመ፲ለየካቲት፡: fol. 99v አመ፡ [፲]ወ፯ለሚያዝያ፡: fol. 111r አመ፡ [n.l.]ወ፯ምንባብ፡: fol. 135r አመ፪ለኀምሌ፡ ምንባብ፡: fol. 137r

binding.decoNote: Two wooden boards covered with reddish-brown tooled leather; textile inlays. Two pairs of sewing stations.
Two pairs of chains of endband stitches at the spine cover. Most of the quires are reinforced by parchment guards.
Blind tooled ornament on the reddish-brown leather cover (at least 6 tools were used: triple line fillet, cross hatching, double circles, swirl, corni-form, small x).

history.provenance: Habta Śǝllāse is mentioned as the commissioner in Additio 2.

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

revisionDesc.change: transformed from mycore to TEI P5
